title: EPIC: API Advanced Features
body:
Epic covering all advanced API enhancements: analytics endpoints, webhooks, audit logging, bulk operations, rate limiting improvements, API versioning, and report generation.

## Scope
- Advanced analytics endpoints with aggregation pipelines
- Webhook system for real-time event notifications
- Comprehensive audit logging
- Bulk CRUD operations
- Enhanced rate limiting per-user
- API v2 versioning strategy
- PDF/CSV report generation

## Success Criteria
- All new endpoints follow existing patterns (route → controller → service)
- Every query filters by userId
- Full test coverage with mongodb-memory-server
- Swagger JSDoc for all new endpoints

title: EPIC: Frontend Enhancements
body:
Epic covering frontend improvements: dashboard redesign, real-time notifications, PWA support, accessibility audit, advanced charting, mobile optimization, and dark mode polish.

## Scope
- Dashboard KPI cards and overview redesign
- WebSocket-based real-time notifications
- Progressive Web App manifest and service worker
- WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit and fixes
- Advanced Recharts visualizations
- Mobile-first responsive improvements
- Dark mode theme polish

## Success Criteria
- All components handle loading/error/empty states
- Sonner toasts on all mutations
- TanStack Query for all data fetching
- shadcn/ui components preferred over custom

title: EPIC: Schema & Types Evolution
body:
Epic for shared-types package evolution: pagination schemas, WebSocket event schemas, API versioning types, bulk operation schemas, report schemas, and schema migration utilities.

## Scope
- Paginated request/response schemas (cursor + offset)
- WebSocket event type schemas
- API v2 response envelope schemas
- Bulk operation request/response schemas
- Report generation schemas
- Webhook payload schemas

## Success Criteria
- All types inferred from Zod schemas (never hand-written)
- Both apps/api and apps/web consume schemas correctly
- Full Vitest coverage

title: EPIC: MCP Server Expansion
body:
Epic for MCP server enhancements: streaming support, batch tool operations, new analytical tools, caching layer, performance optimization, and additional resources.

## Scope
- Server-Sent Events streaming for long-running queries
- Batch tool operations (multi-transaction, multi-account)
- Advanced analytical tools (trend analysis, anomaly detection)
- Redis caching layer for frequent queries
- Tool response pagination
- New resources: spending-trends, category-breakdown, net-worth-history

## Success Criteria
- All tools return standard MCP format
- All queries filter by getUserId()
- Tests with mongodb-memory-server

title: EPIC: Agentic AI Improvements
body:
Epic for AI agent enhancements: persistent conversation memory, multi-agent collaboration, custom user prompts, cost optimization, streaming responses, and new specialist agents.

## Scope
- MongoDB-backed conversation persistence
- Multi-agent collaboration protocol
- User-customizable system prompts
- Token usage optimization and caching
- Streaming chat responses via SSE
- Tax planning specialist agent
- Investment analysis specialist agent

## Success Criteria
- All agents extend BaseAgent
- System prompts in src/prompts/
- Response shape maintained
- Cost tracking per request

title: EPIC: Context Engineering Evolution
body:
Epic for context engine improvements: persistent graph storage, real-time knowledge sync, user-specific context, graph analytics dashboard, and integration with agentic-ai.

## Scope
- MongoDB-backed graph persistence (beyond in-memory)
- Real-time graph updates via WebSocket
- Per-user knowledge subgraphs
- Graph analytics (centrality, clustering)
- Enhanced D3.js visualization
- Deep integration with agentic-ai for context-aware responses

## Success Criteria
- Graph operations maintain existing API
- Vitest coverage for all new features
- D3 visualization works at /ui endpoint

title: EPIC: Infrastructure & DevOps
body:
Epic for infrastructure improvements: CI/CD pipeline, monitoring, security hardening, Kubernetes optimization, Terraform modules, and observability.

## Scope
- GitHub Actions CI with Turbo cache
- Automated deployment pipeline (staging + production)
- Prometheus + Grafana monitoring
- Security headers and CSP
- Kubernetes resource optimization
- Terraform module improvements
- Log aggregation setup

## Success Criteria
- CI runs all tests and type-checks
- Zero-downtime deployments
- All security headers in place

title: EPIC: Testing & Quality
body:
Epic for test coverage expansion: E2E Playwright tests, integration tests, performance benchmarks, accessibility tests, and visual regression testing.

## Scope
- Playwright E2E test suite for critical user journeys
- API integration tests (multi-endpoint workflows)
- Performance benchmarks for API response times
- Lighthouse accessibility scoring
- Visual regression testing with Playwright screenshots
- Load testing with k6

## Success Criteria
- E2E covers login → dashboard → transaction → budget flow
- API benchmarks establish baselines
- Accessibility score > 90

title: EPIC: Data & Analytics
body:
Epic for data features: bank statement import, data export, ML-based categorization, advanced reporting, and financial insights engine.

## Scope
- Multi-format bank import (CSV, OFX, QIF)
- Data export (CSV, PDF, JSON)
- ML-based transaction auto-categorization
- Custom report builder
- Spending trend analysis
- Net worth tracking over time

## Success Criteria
- Import handles major bank CSV formats
- Export includes date range filtering
- Categorization accuracy > 85%

title: EPIC: User Experience
body:
Epic for UX improvements: onboarding flow, help system, multi-currency support, notification preferences, keyboard shortcuts, and settings page.

## Scope
- First-time user onboarding wizard
- Contextual help tooltips and guide
- Multi-currency with exchange rate API
- Notification preferences (email, push, in-app)
- Keyboard navigation shortcuts
- Comprehensive settings/preferences page

## Success Criteria
- Onboarding completes in < 3 minutes
- Help available on every major page
- Currency conversion accurate to 4 decimal places

title: Add pagination schemas to shared-types
body:
Create reusable pagination schemas supporting both cursor-based and offset-based pagination.

## Context
Multiple API endpoints need consistent pagination. This is a foundation bead — 12+ downstream beads depend on it.

## What to Do
1. Create `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/pagination.schema.ts`
2. Export `PaginatedRequestSchema` with:
   - `page` (number, optional, default 1)
   - `limit` (number, optional, default 20, max 100)
   - `cursor` (string, optional — for cursor-based)
   - `sortBy` (string, optional)
   - `sortOrder` (enum: 'asc' | 'desc', default 'desc')
3. Export `PaginatedResponseSchema<T>` with:
   - `data` (array of T)
   - `pagination` object: `{ total, page, limit, totalPages, hasNext, hasPrev, nextCursor? }`
4. Add inferred types to `types/index.ts`
5. Write comprehensive tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Validates page >= 1, limit 1-100
- Cursor and offset modes coexist
- Types correctly inferred
- Tests cover all edge cases

## Files to Modify
- `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/pagination.schema.ts` (new)
- `packages/shared-types/src/types/index.ts`
- `packages/shared-types/src/__tests__/pagination.test.ts` (new)

title: Add bulk operation schemas to shared-types
body:
Create Zod schemas for bulk CRUD operations across all entities.

## Context
Users need to perform mass operations (bulk delete transactions, bulk categorize, bulk import). These schemas define the request/response contract.

## What to Do
1. Create `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/bulk.schema.ts`
2. Export schemas:
   - `BulkCreateSchema<T>` — array of creation payloads
   - `BulkUpdateSchema` — array of { id, updates } pairs
   - `BulkDeleteSchema` — array of IDs
   - `BulkResultSchema` — { succeeded: number, failed: number, errors: Array<{ id, message }> }
3. Add specific bulk schemas per entity (BulkCreateTransactionsSchema, etc.)
4. Add types to `types/index.ts`
5. Write tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Max 500 items per bulk operation
- Error details per failed item
- Types inferred from Zod

## Files to Modify
- `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/bulk.schema.ts` (new)
- `packages/shared-types/src/types/index.ts`
- `packages/shared-types/src/__tests__/bulk.test.ts` (new)

title: Add webhook event schemas to shared-types
body:
Create Zod schemas for webhook event payloads.

## Context
The webhook system (br-031) needs typed event payloads. Define them in shared-types so both API and consumers share the same contract.

## What to Do
1. Create `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/webhook.schema.ts`
2. Define event types: transaction.created, transaction.updated, budget.exceeded, goal.reached, recurring.processed, account.balance_changed
3. Each event has: id, type, timestamp, userId, payload (discriminated union by type)
4. Export `WebhookEventSchema`, `WebhookConfigSchema` (URL, events, secret, active)
5. Tests for all event types

## Acceptance Criteria
- Discriminated union validates correct payload per event type
- Config schema validates webhook URLs
- All types inferred

## Files to Modify
- `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/webhook.schema.ts` (new)
- `packages/shared-types/src/types/index.ts`
- `packages/shared-types/src/__tests__/webhook.test.ts` (new)

title: Add report schemas to shared-types
body:

1. Create `packages/shared-types/src/schemas/report.schema.ts`
2. Define: `ReportRequestSchema` (dateRange, type, format, filters)
3. Report types: spending-summary, income-vs-expense, category-breakdown, net-worth, tax-summary
4. Output formats: json, csv, pdf
5. `ReportResponseSchema` with metadata + data sections

## Acceptance Criteria
- Date range validation (start < end, max 1 year)
- Filter by accounts, categories

title: API: Advanced analytics endpoints
body:
Add new analytics endpoints with MongoDB aggregation pipelines for spending trends, category breakdowns, and comparative analysis.

## Context
The existing analytics are basic. Users need trend analysis, month-over-month comparisons, and category drill-down. Depends on pagination schemas (br-011).

## What to Do
1. Add to `apps/api/src/services/analytics.service.ts`:
   - `getSpendingTrends(userId, dateRange, granularity)` — daily/weekly/monthly aggregation
   - `getCategoryBreakdown(userId, dateRange, accountIds?)` — pie chart data
   - `getIncomeVsExpense(userId, dateRange, granularity)` — bar chart data
   - `getTopMerchants(userId, dateRange, limit)` — top spending merchants
   - `getMonthOverMonth(userId)` — current vs previous month comparison
2. Controller + routes following existing patterns
3. Swagger JSDoc for all endpoints
4. Tests with mongodb-memory-server

## Acceptance Criteria
- All queries filter by userId
- Paginated where applicable
- Aggregation pipelines optimized with proper indexes
- Response time < 500ms for 10k transactions

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/services/analytics.service.ts`
- `apps/api/src/controllers/analytics.controller.ts`
- `apps/api/src/routes/analytics.route.ts`
- `apps/api/src/__tests__/services/analytics.service.test.ts`

title: API: Paginated list endpoints
body:
Retrofit all existing list endpoints to use the new pagination schemas.

## Context
Currently list endpoints return unbounded arrays. With pagination schemas ready (br-011), add cursor and offset support to all list services.

## What to Do
1. Update `transaction.service.ts` — paginated list with filters
2. Update `account.service.ts` — paginated list
3. Update `budget.service.ts` — paginated list
4. Update `goal.service.ts` — paginated list
5. Update `recurring.service.ts` — paginated list
6. Update `category.service.ts` — paginated list
7. Update all corresponding controllers and routes
8. Update tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- All list endpoints accept page, limit, sortBy, sortOrder
- Response includes pagination metadata
- Backward compatible (defaults to page 1, limit 20)
- Tests cover pagination edge cases (last page, empty results)

## Files to Modify
- All service files in `apps/api/src/services/`
- All controller files
- All route files
- All test files

title: API: Bulk transaction operations
body:
Add bulk create, update, and delete endpoints for transactions.

## Context
Users importing bank statements or doing mass cleanup need bulk operations. Depends on bulk schemas (br-012).

## What to Do
1. Add to `transaction.service.ts`:
   - `bulkCreate(userId, transactions[])` — insert many, return per-item results
   - `bulkUpdate(userId, updates[])` — update many by ID
   - `bulkDelete(userId, ids[])` — soft delete many
2. Add controller methods and routes: POST /bulk, PATCH /bulk, DELETE /bulk
3. Limit: 500 items per request
4. Return BulkResult with succeeded/failed counts and per-item errors
5. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Max 500 items enforced
- Partial success supported (some succeed, some fail)
- All items filtered by userId
- Audit trail per bulk operation

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/services/transaction.service.ts`
- `apps/api/src/controllers/transaction.controller.ts`
- `apps/api/src/routes/transaction.route.ts`
- `apps/api/src/__tests__/services/transaction.service.test.ts`

title: API: Audit logging middleware and service
body:
Implement comprehensive audit logging for all write operations.

## Context
For compliance and debugging, every create/update/delete should be logged with before/after state. Depends on audit schemas (br-019).

## What to Do
1. Create `apps/api/src/models/audit-log.model.ts` — Mongoose schema
2. Create `apps/api/src/services/audit.service.ts` — create, query, aggregate audit logs
3. Create `apps/api/src/middleware/audit.middleware.ts` — captures before/after state
4. Add audit query endpoint: GET /audit-logs with filters
5. Apply middleware to all write routes
6. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Captures userId, action, entity, entityId, before/after diff
- Does not block the main request (fire-and-forget or queue)
- Queryable by action, entity, date range
- Retention: 90 days default

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/models/audit-log.model.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/services/audit.service.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/middleware/audit.middleware.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/routes/audit.route.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/controllers/audit.controller.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/app.ts` (register middleware)

title: API: Webhook system for event notifications
body:
Implement a webhook system that notifies external services of financial events.

## Context
Depends on webhook schemas (br-013). Enables integrations with Slack, Zapier, IFTTT, etc.

## What to Do
1. Create `apps/api/src/models/webhook.model.ts` — url, events[], secret, active, userId
2. Create `apps/api/src/services/webhook.service.ts`:
   - CRUD for webhook configs
   - `dispatch(userId, event)` — fan out to matching webhooks
   - HMAC signature with webhook secret
   - Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)
   - Delivery log
3. Controller + routes: CRUD /webhooks, GET /webhooks/:id/deliveries
4. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Webhook-Signature header
- 3 retries with exponential backoff
- Delivery log with status, response code, latency
- Max 10 webhooks per user

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/models/webhook.model.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/services/webhook.service.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/controllers/webhook.controller.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/routes/webhook.route.ts` (new)

title: API: Bank statement import (CSV, OFX)
body:
Implement multi-format bank statement import with column mapping.

## Context
Depends on import schemas (br-018). Users upload bank files and map columns to transaction fields.

## What to Do
1. Create `apps/api/src/services/import.service.ts`
2. Support CSV (PapaParse) and OFX (ofx-js) formats
3. Column mapping: auto-detect common bank formats (Chase, BofA, Amex, Wells Fargo)
4. Duplicate detection: match by date + amount + description
5. Preview mode: parse and return first 5 rows without importing
6. Bulk create via transaction service
7. Controller + route: POST /import/preview, POST /import/execute
8. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Auto-detects 4+ common bank CSV formats
- Duplicate detection prevents re-import
- Preview shows mapped data before commit
- Returns import result with success/skip/error counts

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/services/import.service.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/controllers/import.controller.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/routes/import.route.ts` (new)

2. Create `apps/api/src/services/notification.service.ts`:

   - `list(userId, filters)` — paginated, filter by read/unread/type
   - `markRead(userId, id)` — mark single as read
   - `markAllRead(userId)` — mark all as read
   - `getUnreadCount(userId)` — badge count
3. Trigger notifications from: budget exceeded, goal milestone, recurring processed
4. Controller + routes: GET /notifications, PATCH /notifications/:id/read, etc.

title: API: Multi-currency support with exchange rates
body:
Add multi-currency transaction support with real-time exchange rates.

## What to Do
1. Add currency field to transaction model (already exists)
2. Create `apps/api/src/services/exchange-rate.service.ts`:
   - Fetch rates from free API (exchangerate-api.com or frankfurter.app)
   - Cache rates for 1 hour
   - `convert(amount, from, to, date?)` — historical or current
3. Update analytics to normalize to user's base currency
4. Tests with mocked exchange rate API

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports 30+ currencies
- Rate caching (1 hour TTL)
- Historical rates for past transactions
- Analytics normalize to base currency

title: API: WebSocket server for real-time updates
body:
Add WebSocket support to the Express API for real-time push notifications.

## Context
Depends on WebSocket event schemas (br-016). Enables real-time dashboard updates.

## What to Do
1. Add `ws` package to apps/api
2. Create `apps/api/src/services/websocket.service.ts`:
   - Connection management per userId
   - Authentication via JWT in connection handshake
   - Event broadcasting: transaction.new, budget.update, notification, balance.change
3. Integrate with transaction/budget/notification services to emit events
4. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT auth on WebSocket handshake
- Per-user channels (no cross-user data)
- Automatic reconnection handling
- Heartbeat ping/pong

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/services/websocket.service.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/app.ts` (mount WS server)
- `apps/api/src/services/transaction.service.ts` (emit events)
- `apps/api/src/services/notification.service.ts` (emit events)

title: API: ML-based transaction auto-categorization
body:
Implement intelligent auto-categorization for uncategorized transactions.

## What to Do
1. Create `apps/api/src/services/categorization.service.ts`
2. Approach: TF-IDF on transaction descriptions + user's historical category assignments
3. Training: build model from user's existing categorized transactions
4. Prediction: suggest category + confidence for new transactions
5. Endpoint: POST /transactions/categorize (batch of transaction IDs)
6. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Accuracy > 85% on test data with 100+ categorized transactions
- Returns confidence score per suggestion
- Falls back to 'Uncategorized' below 50% confidence
- Per-user model (no cross-user training data)

## Files to Modify
- `apps/api/src/services/categorization.service.ts` (new)
- `apps/api/src/controllers/transaction.controller.ts` (add endpoint)
- `apps/api/src/routes/transaction.route.ts` (add route)

title: API: Health check and readiness endpoints

1. Create `apps/api/src/routes/health.route.ts`
2. GET /health — liveness (always 200 if process is up)
3. GET /ready — readiness (checks MongoDB connection, returns 503 if not connected)
4. GET /health/detailed — full status of all dependencies (MongoDB, optional Redis)
5. Tests

title: AI: Persistent conversation memory with MongoDB
body:
Replace in-memory conversation storage with MongoDB-backed persistence.

## Context
Currently conversations are lost on server restart. This is the foundation for multi-session AI interactions.

## What to Do
1. Create `agentic-ai/src/models/conversation.model.ts` — Mongoose schema
2. Fields: userId, conversationId, messages[], agent, metadata, createdAt, updatedAt
3. Update `conversation/state.ts` to use MongoDB instead of Map
4. Add TTL: conversations expire after 30 days of inactivity
5. Index on userId + conversationId
6. Tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conversations persist across server restarts
- Loads previous context on session resume
- TTL cleanup of stale conversations
- All queries filter by userId

## Files to Modify
- `agentic-ai/src/models/conversation.model.ts` (new)
- `agentic-ai/src/conversation/state.ts`
- `agentic-ai/src/__tests__/conversation.test.ts`

2. POST /agent/chat/stream — returns SSE stream
3. Stream Claude's response tokens as they arrive
4. Include tool_use events in stream (so frontend can show 'Looking up your transactions...')
5. Final event includes full response + usage stats

## Acceptance Criteria
- First token < 500ms
- Tool use intermediate events visible
- Final event has complete response
- Error handling: stream error event if tool fails
